- SRDTrans uses spatial redundancy sampling (spatially adjacent pixels) and a lightweight transformer architecture, making it effective even at low imaging speeds (e.g., 0.3 Hz).
- The self-inspired model (SN2N) uses diagonal resampling and self-constrained learning, focusing on spatial redundancy and denoising without relying on temporal redundancy.
- SRDTrans is explicitly shown to work well at low frame rates (0.3 Hz), unlike methods relying on temporal redundancy.
- SRDTrans does not require temporal sampling; it uses spatial redundancy, which is more suitable for low-speed imaging.
- SRDTrans is efficient and effective at low frame rates, not limited by dynamic training data.
- The self-inspired model (SN2N) is not stated to have fewer parameters or better generalization than SRDTrans.
- Option A is incorrect because both models use spatial redundancy, not exclusively one.
- Option B is false — SRDTrans is designed for low frame rates.

- Option D is not supported by the text.
